deal_flags.reason: target counsel: Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP
deal_flags.source_quote: Members of management and representatives of each of GHF and the Company’s legal counsel, Hinckley, Allen & Snyder LLP (which we refer to as “Hinckley Allen”), participated in all meetings of the Transaction Committee, except as otherwise indicated.

deal_flags.reason: acquirer counsel: Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP
deal_flags.source_quote: On August 10, 2016, representatives of Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher & Bartlett LLP (which we refer to as “Simpson Thacher”), G&W’s legal counsel, discussed various provisions of the draft merger agreement and voting agreement that had been provided to G&W on August 3, 2016, and Hinckley Allen provided Simpson Thacher with a revised disclosure letter to accompany the August 3 draft merger agreement.

deal_flags.reason: termination fee $3.785 million; background also states negotiated to 3% of transaction value
deal_flags.source_quote: the Board is permitted to terminate the merger agreement in order to enter into a definitive agreement for that superior proposal, subject to certain notice requirements, “matching rights” and payment of a termination fee of $3.785 million to G&W;

events.source_quote: In the fourth quarter of 2015, Robert H. Eder (the Company’s Chairman and Chief Executive Officer) and Frank K. Rogers (the Company’s Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer) met with one of the Company’s Class I rail partners (“Party A”) to discuss joint commercial opportunities. During the meeting, Party A suggested possible joint venture arrangements and expressed some interest in acquiring equity in the Company to further develop strategic opportunities and enhance regional connectivity.

flags.reason: phrase: 'fourth quarter of 2015'

events.source_quote: During the executive session of the Board’s regular quarterly meeting held on January 27, 2016 (which Director Alfred P. Smith did not attend), the subcommittee recommended to the Board that the Company retain GHF as its investment banking firm. At that meeting, representatives of GHF discussed with the Board its qualifications and a proposed process should the directors ultimately decide to proceed with a change in control transaction.

At a special meeting of the Board held on March 14, 2016, representatives of GHF discussed their evaluation of the Company’s business operations and strategy for improving operational and financial performance, as well as railroad industry dynamics more generally and their impact on current railroad valuations. Representatives of GHF then proposed a process pursuant to which the Company would proceed with discussions with Party A and also solicit interest from other potential third parties regarding a change in control transaction.

During the week of March 28, 2016, in accordance with the Transaction Committee’s directives, representatives of GHF contacted 11 potential strategic buyers (including Party A) and 18 potential financial buyers. Each of the potential strategic buyers and 14 potential financial buyers subsequently executed confidentiality agreements.

flags.reason: phrase: 'During the week of March 28, 2016'; midpoint: 2016-03-30

events.additional_note: Initial non-binding IOI deadline announced; deadline later postponed to May 19, 2016.
events.source_quote: the Board met in executive session (without Mr. Eder present) with representatives of GHF and discussed the status of the process, including that each potential buyer had been advised to submit a non-binding indication of interest by May 10, 2016 (which deadline was subsequently postponed to May 19, 2016 to ensure the potential buyers had sufficient time to formulate their most compelling offers).

flags.reason: non-binding IOI deadline inferred as an informal round announcement

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: filing calls these non-binding IOIs and gives only a collective price range.
events.additional_note: Collective range across nine IOIs; filing does not allocate prices to individual bidders.
events.source_quote: Between May 19, 2016 and June 1, 2016, the Company received nine written indications of interest (“IOIs”) from potential buyers, with offer prices per share ranging from $17.93 to $26.50 (assuming the conversion of preferred stock into common stock), implying equity values of $90 million to $134 million. The Transaction Committee met by telephone conference on May 23, 2016 and again on June 1, 2016 to consider the IOIs. In view of the substantial amount of management time that would be required for management presentations, the Transaction Committee concluded that the two low bidders should be excluded from that process.

events.additional_note: Non-binding LOI round with July 20 deadline and draft transaction documents.
events.source_quote: In mid-June 2016, at the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement, which GHF indicated would be posted to the internet data site. Potential buyers were instructed to provide a valuation for the Company based on the purchase of 100% of the outstanding common stock on a dollar per share basis (assuming conversion of the preferred stock into common stock) or the financial terms of any non-cash proposal.

flags.reason: phrase: 'mid-June 2016'
non-binding LOI deadline inferred as an informal final-round announcement

events.source_quote: In early July 2016, a potential strategic buyer that had not previously been part of the process (“Party C”) approached the Company’s management and expressed interest in acquiring the Company. After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C was provided the memorandum concerning the Company provided to the other potential buyers. On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million).

flags.reason: phrase: 'early July 2016'

After executing a confidentiality agreement, Party C was provided the memorandum concerning the Company provided to the other potential buyers. On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million).

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: pre-LOI IOI submitted after NDA and before final transaction negotiations.

On July 12, 2016, Party C submitted an IOI with an offer price per share of $21.00 (implying an equity value of $108 million).

events.additional_note: Deadline for non-binding LOIs announced in mid-June.
events.source_quote: In mid-June 2016, at the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF instructed all potential buyers to submit non-binding letters of intent (“LOIs”) by July 20, 2016, together with mark-ups of the forms of merger agreement, disclosure letter and voting agreement, which GHF indicated would be posted to the internet data site. Potential buyers were instructed to provide a valuation for the Company based on the purchase of 100% of the outstanding common stock on a dollar per share basis (assuming conversion of the preferred stock into common stock) or the financial terms of any non-cash proposal.

flags.reason: deadline inferred from LOI instruction language

events.process_conditions_note: Subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: LOI was in a non-binding LOI round and subject to exclusivity/diligence.
events.additional_note: CVR based on subsequent sale of the South Quay property.
events.source_quote: • G&W submitted an LOI on July 21, 2016 to acquire the Company for a price of $21.15 per share (subject to a three-week exclusive diligence period). The price included $20.02 cash at closing and $1.13 in the form of a contingent value right, the value of which was based on the subsequent sale of the Company’s South Quay property (“CVR”).

events.process_conditions_note: Subject to an expedited diligence review.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: LOI in non-binding LOI round and subject to expedited diligence.
events.source_quote: • Party B submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $24.00 per share (subject to an expedited diligence review).

flags.reason: phrase: 'late July 2016'

events.process_conditions_note: Subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation.

events.source_quote: • Party E (a strategic buyer) submitted an LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $21.26 per share (subject to a 60-day exclusivity period for due diligence and negotiation of definitive documentation).

events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: revised LOI remained part of non-binding LOI process and retained CVR structure.
events.additional_note: Revised from July 21 LOI after feedback that price and CVR structure were not competitive.
events.source_quote: On July 26, 2016, in response to feedback from representatives of GHF indicating its price and CVR structure were not competitive, G&W submitted a revised LOI, which increased its offer to $22.15 per share (including $21.02 in cash and $1.13 CVR).

events.additional_note: Confirmatory diligence and negotiation phase narrowed to G&W and Party B.
events.source_quote: The Transaction Committee concluded that the Company should proceed with confirmatory due diligence and negotiations with G&W and Party B because of the higher offers made by each of G&W and Party B relative to the other potential buyers. At the direction of the Transaction Committee, representatives of GHF subsequently contacted the remaining bidders to inform them that they were no longer involved in the process.

events.source_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.

flags.reason: Party D submitted revised LOI after July 29 re-engagement call

events.process_conditions_note: Contained a 30-day due diligence period; financing support from Party F.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1: revised LOI still required a 30-day diligence period.
events.additional_note: Party E revised LOI included financing support from Party F.
events.source_quote: On August 1, 2016, Party D submitted a revised LOI at a price of $24.00 per share and Party E submitted a revised LOI, along with financing support, from Party F, at a price of $23.81 per share. Both revised proposals contained a 30-day due diligence period.

flags.reason: Party E submitted revised LOI after July 29 re-engagement call

events.additional_note: Withdrew revised proposal but confirmed original $21.26 proposal.
events.source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share) and Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.

events.additional_note: Would not proceed with further diligence after Company declined requested standstill commitment.
events.source_quote: Party E withdrew its revised proposal on August 2, 2016 (but confirmed its original proposal of $21.26 per share) and Party D declined to shorten its due diligence period and requested that the Company commit that it would not enter into an agreement with another bidder during its 30-day due diligence period. In light of the active interest of Party B and G&W, the Company declined to make such a commitment, at which point Party D indicated that it would not proceed with further due diligence at that time.

events.process_conditions_note: Offer expired at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.
events.bid_type_inference_note: Formal per §G1: final revised LOI with merger-agreement markups, cash price, short expiry, and same-day execution.
events.source_quote: On the morning of August 12, 2016, following a discussion between representatives of BMO and G&W in which G&W indicated it would be submitting a revised LOI, G&W submitted a revised LOI to acquire the Company for a price of $25.00 per share in cash, which excluded the previously proposed CVR, together with mark-ups of the merger agreement and the voting agreement. G&W’s LOI further indicated that the offer would expire at 6:00 p.m. on the following day.

events.additional_note: Party B declined to increase its $24.00 price after G&W submitted $25.00 cash offer.
events.source_quote: At the request of the Board, representatives of BMO then contacted Party B to determine if it would increase its offer price. Party B indicated that it would not increase its price.

events.additional_note: Merger agreement executed August 12; public announcement issued August 15 before NASDAQ open.
events.source_quote: Shortly thereafter, Hinckley Allen and Simpson Thacher finalized the transaction documents and the Company and G&W executed the merger agreement, and the Company, G&W and the Eder Trusts executed the voting agreement. The Company issued a press release publicly announcing the transaction on Monday, August 15, 2016 prior to the opening of trading of the Company’s common stock on the NASDAQ.
